prompt:
I run the online side of our Saturday farm market and since we shipped the "reorder last week's basket" button the support inbox is a mess. Two flavors of weird, and I can reproduce both:

1) A customer hits reorder on a saved basket, gets a fresh copy to tweak, adds a couple of jars of honey to the COPY — and the honey shows up in the saved original too. Quantity bumps do the same thing: bump eggs from 2 to 5 in the copy, the saved basket now says 5. Remove sourdough from the copy, it vanishes from the original. It's like the two baskets are possessed; whatever you do to one happens to the other. I first blamed the browser cache, but it reproduces server-side in the test suite with no browser anywhere.

2) Order history is useless now. Every order in the history screen shows the right order total but an EMPTY item list — and one customer swears her history briefly showed the flowers she added for NEXT week inside LAST week's order. The money math is right, the items are gone or wrong.

Our acceptance suite (CartServiceTests.cs) fails on exactly these behaviors, same failures every run — `dotnet test` to see it. Please find what's actually wrong in the service and fix it properly. Don't touch CartServiceTests.cs, MarketCart.csproj, or .gitignore; the expectations in there are exactly how the market staff expect baskets and history to behave.
